trigger_reason: step 13 FIRED for R814; step 13.5 v11 adversarial attack triggered.

step_13_spec_pre_check_input.rationale: SO(3)-equivariant attention modifies the score function so that s(q_i, k_j) is invariant under simultaneous SO(3) rotation of (q,k); equivariance under hidden-state rotation. Slot S01 modification.

attacks.claim: SO(3) rotation at small g reduces to identity; the equivariant scoring becomes ordinary attention.
attacks.evidence: Standard small-rotation argument: exp(g X) approx I + g X; equivariant score approx q^T k (baseline) for small rotation.
attacks.rebuttal: SO(3) is a 3-dimensional compact Lie group with non-trivial structure. The equivariance is enforced by a training-time penalty that maintains non-zero rotation magnitude. The structural distinguishability is at the score-symmetry level: the score s(q,k) is invariant under rotating (q,k) by any g in SO(3) -- this is a STRUCTURAL constraint that ordinary attention lacks. Reducing to ordinary attention requires setting all rotations to zero, which the training penalty prevents.

attacks.claim: 3-seed paired t-test on LRA Pathfinder/WMT16 has ~50% power
attacks.evidence: Standard power calculation; LRA std approx 0.8 percentage points

attacks.claim: SE(2)-equivariant transformers exist (Cohen 2018); SO(3) not novel
attacks.rebuttal: SE(2)-equivariance is for 2D images; SO(3) is for 3D rotational structure. The attention-score equivariance is at the SCORE level (s(q,k) is rotation-invariant), which is distinct from SE(2)/SO(3)-equivariant convolutions.

rationale: R814 SO(3)-equivariant attention scoring: post-attack distinguishability TRUE (architectural-distinguishability preserved via the rebuttal arguments).
